description:
## Background

Once Issue Explorer state is represented as a renderer route, Beadsmith needs browser-style session navigation. Wouter observes History API changes but does not define Beadsmith’s semantic Issue Navigation Entries, custom toolbar boundaries, destination labels, or keyboard policy.

Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## What to build

Add Back and Forward navigation for Issue destinations within the Current Workspace. Deliberate Issue and Issue List View changes create entries, search edits replace the current entry, and users can traverse the exact view/search/selection context using persistent controls or platform shortcuts.

## Technical Approach

- Introduce one focused navigation coordinator around Wouter and the browser History API.
- Define a serializable Issue Navigation Entry containing Workspace path, optional Issue ID, Issue List View, Issue Search, and a Beadsmith navigation index. Store no Issue data.
- Initialize the renderer root as index 0. Increment indexes for pushes, retain the index for replacement, and truncate the in-memory forward ledger after pushing from a historical entry.
- Keep a session-only ledger keyed by navigation index so toolbar boundaries and adjacent destination tooltips are reliable; browser history remains the source of traversal events.
- Push exactly once when selecting a different Issue or changing Issue List View. Selecting the current Issue and selecting the current view are no-ops.
- Replace the current entry for every search edit while preserving its selected Issue.
- Add persistent Back and Forward buttons, disabled at Beadsmith’s own boundaries and labelled/tooltipped with the adjacent destination when available.
- Add Command–Left Bracket and Command–Right Bracket shortcuts on macOS and Alt–Left and Alt–Right on Linux/Windows. Do not consume ordinary text-input behavior.
- On traversal, restore view, search, and optional selected Issue as one destination, move focus to the Issue Detail heading or landmark after resolution, and start Issue Detail at the top.
- Keep all navigation state session-only; do not add store persistence.

## Considerations

- `history.length` cannot determine Beadsmith’s boundaries. Use the entry index and session ledger rather than unrelated webview history.
- Forward history must be discarded after a new push from the middle of the ledger.
- Programmatic URL normalization and search updates must not accidentally push entries.
- This ticket handles only entries whose Workspace path matches the Current Workspace. Cross-Workspace traversal is a later child.
- Shortcut detection must account for platform modifiers and editable targets without breaking accessibility.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Selecting a different Issue pushes one indexed Issue Navigation Entry.
- [ ] Changing Issue List View pushes one entry and applies the agreed selection-clearing rule.
- [ ] Search edits replace the current entry and do not grow history.
- [ ] Selecting the current Issue or view does not create a duplicate entry.
- [ ] Back and Forward restore Issue List View, Issue Search, and selected Issue together.
- [ ] Pushing after Back discards the prior forward destinations.
- [ ] Persistent controls expose accurate enabled states and adjacent destination tooltips.
- [ ] macOS and Linux/Windows shortcuts invoke the same commands without disrupting editable controls.
- [ ] Traversal focuses the destination detail heading/landmark and starts detail at the top.
- [ ] Navigation entries are not restored after application restart.

## Test Plan

- Add pure coordinator tests for push, replace, duplicate suppression, index progression, boundary calculation, and forward truncation.
- Add renderer integration tests using real browser history events for controls, keyboard shortcuts, view/search/Issue restoration, focus, and titles.
- Test editable-target shortcut behavior explicitly.
- Run renderer tests, TypeScript build, Ultracite, and existing application navigation tests.
title: Add same-Workspace Back and Forward navigation
